symptoms.symptom_name: Severe widespread pain flare of known primary centralized pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Widespread pain flare on a chronic centralized background fits central sensitization.
Diffuse allodynia is a hallmark of central pain amplification.
Sleep deprivation both drives and results from central sensitization, amplifying pain and fog.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, neck stiffness, photophobia or vomiting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever, meningism and vomiting argues strongly against CNS infection, though it cannot be fully excluded remotely.
Afebrile with normal breathing lowers sepsis probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cognitive fog: word-finding difficulty, timeline recall failure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: 'Fibro-fog' is a recognised feature, but new cognitive change still requires organic exclusion.
Cognitive change is non-specific and cannot alone distinguish benign fog from early encephalopathy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Long-standing identical but milder cognitive fog with prior flares

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A consistent prior pattern supports a flare rather than a new organic process.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rousable, oriented to person and place (per bystander)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved rousability and basic orientation argue against significant encephalopathy, though formal cognition needs in-person assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tender-point tenderness without effusion or erythema (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Widespread tender points with no joint inflammation support centralized pain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cranial nerves intact, no nuchal rigidity (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intact cranial nerves and no meningism on exam argue against CNS infection.
Established fibromyalgia is the substrate for a central sensitization flare.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Diffuse allodynia, total insomnia and word-finding/timeline fog, with a clean infective/meningism screen, preserved rousability and a consistent prior pattern, indicate a severe sleep-deprived central sensitization flare rather than CNS infection — but the new confusion still mandates in-person exclusion of organic causes.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person neurological and cognitive assessment (GCS, meningism, cranial nerves)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Required to exclude CNS infection/encephalopathy underlying new confusion; cannot be done via chat.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Basic bloods (FBC, CRP, electrolytes, glucose, calcium, LFTs)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude metabolic/septic causes of confusion.
Only if in-person assessment raises concern for CNS infection; not a first remote step.
